metadata.title:
A BIT OF HISTORY.

CONCERNING TIE MARVELLOUS


GROWTH OF

EILERs PIANO HOUSE.

Three Years OF Unprecedented Sue
oess-Active and Progressive
Effort Establishes Mann


moth Piano Business


in Spokane.

pages.raw_text:
Our first move in Spokane was to
5. Johnston company, who had a mu-
sic house on Riverside avenue. The
business was newly organized assthe
Inland Empire Music company" and
later as the Eilers Piano House See
ing the vast field and golden oppor-
tunity prevailing here for thorough
ly reliable, progressive and up to date
music concern, with ample capital. we
concluded to make it a permanent In
stitution and merge the business with
that of our other great stores at
Portland, San Francisco and Sacra-
mento.


We commenced advertising the low
prices and easy terms at which we
sell pianos, such as We're never before
heard of in Spokane, and as a result
the patronage extended to us was lib
eral from the first.


few months later several local
dealers went out of business. and we
bought up their stocks at so cents on
the dollar and sold them at special
sale at proportionately great reduc-
tions.


BY this time business was crowding
us so that We were compelled to rent
a larger storeroom. We left our place
on Riverside avenue and moved to
605-601 Sprague avenue on January !,
1901, where we still remain, and
where We have over 10000 square feet
Of floor space filled with piaons and
organs. -


Early in the spring of 1903, after
months of negotiating, we secured the
exclusive agency for the celebrated
Pianola for the entire Pacific north
west, and builf>a beautiful parlor in
which to show these wonderful little
instruments. For the convenience of
our pianola customers we are install
ing a library of 10000 pieces of Pia-
nola music.


Since opening up our fine store on
Sprague avenue nearly all the promi-
nent musicians, colleges, public
schools, lodges and churches of Spo-
